message:
feat(test): add core↔vendored async contract verification suite (ft-e34d9.10.5.4)

33 tests enforcing all 12 ABC (Async Boundary Contract) invariants:
- Ownership: task spawner retention, scope bounding, no detached spawns
- Cancellation: propagation deadline, drop-implies-cancel, compat timeout
- Channeling: runtime_compat wrappers, non-lossy close, no raw tokio channels
- Error mapping: vendored→core From impls, context preservation
- Backpressure: semaphore-based concurrency control in mux_pool
- Timeout: Cx/Budget threading, core override of vendored timeouts
- Task lifecycle: JoinHandle tracking, no detached production paths

Static analysis drift detection reads vendored source at compile time.
Serde roundtrip tests for contract types.
Regression anchors: 12 contracts, 7 categories, ABC-XXX-NNN format.

E2e: test_ft_e34d9_10_5_4_contract_verification.sh (10/10 pass)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. +CORRELATION_ID="ft-e34d9.10.5.4-${RUN_ID}"
+LOG_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/contract_verification_${RUN_ID}.jsonl"
+LOG_FILE_REL="${LOG_FILE#"${ROOT_DIR}"/}"
+
+emit_log() {
+  local outcome="$1" decision_path="$2" reason_code="$3" error_code="$4" input_summary="$5"
+  local ts; ts="$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")"
+  jq -cn \
+    --arg ts "${ts}" --arg component "contract_verification.e2e" \
+    --arg sid "${SCENARIO_ID}" --arg cid "${CORRELATION_ID}" \
+    --arg dp "${decision_path}" --arg is "${input_summary}" \
+    --arg oc "${outcome}" --arg rc "${reason_code}" --arg ec "${error_code}" \
+    '{timestamp:$ts,component:$component,scenario_id:$sid,correlation_id:$cid,
+      decision_path:$dp,input_summary:$is,outcome:$oc,reason_code:$rc,error_code:$ec}' \
+    >> "${LOG_FILE}"
+}
+
+echo "=== Core↔vendored async contract verification (ft-e34d9.10.5.4) ==="
+echo "Run ID: ${RUN_ID}"
+echo "Log:    ${LOG_FILE_REL}"
+echo ""
+
+PASS=0; FAIL=0
+TEST_FILE="${ROOT_DIR}/crates/frankenterm-core/tests/vendored_async_contract_verification.rs"
+CONTRACT_FILE="${ROOT_DIR}/crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored_async_contracts.rs"
+
+# S1: Verification test file exists
+echo -n "S1: Contract verification test file exists... "
+if [ -f "${TEST_FILE}" ]; then
+  echo "PASS"; emit_log "pass" "test_file" "exists" "" ""; PASS=$((PASS+1))
+else
+  echo "FAIL"; emit_log "fail" "test_file" "missing" "E_FILE" ""; FAIL=$((FAIL+1))
+fi
+
+# S2: Contract definition file exists
+echo -n "S2: Contract definition file exists... "
+if [ -f "${CONTRACT_FILE}" ]; then
+  echo "PASS"; emit_log "pass" "contract_file" "exists" "" ""; PASS=$((PASS+1))
+else
+  echo "FAIL"; emit_log "fail" "contract_file" "missing" "E_FILE" ""; FAIL=$((FAIL+1))
+fi
+
+# S3: Test count >= 25
+echo -n "S3: Verification test count... "
+TEST_COUNT=$(grep -c '#\[test\]' "${TEST_FILE}" || true)

+  echo "PASS (${TEST_COUNT} tests)"; emit_log "pass" "test_count" "sufficient" "" "count=${TEST_COUNT}"; PASS=$((PASS+1))

+else

+# S6: Serde roundtrip tests present

+
+# S7: Contract evidence infrastructure tested
+echo -n "S7: Contract evidence infrastructure... "
+EVIDENCE=$(grep -c 'ContractCompliance::from_evidence\|ContractEvidence' "${TEST_FILE}" || true)

+echo ""
+echo "=== Results: ${PASS} passed, ${FAIL} failed ==="
+echo "Log: ${LOG_FILE_REL}"
+
+[ "${FAIL}" -gt 0 ] && exit 1 || exit 0
